query: For daily calories [1850,1920,2010,1780,1960,2050,1890,1830,1975,2020], check normality and then run a one-sample t-test versus 2000 only if normality passes. Step 1: call perform_normality_test tool to verify whether a normality assumption is reasonable. Step 2: if normality passes, call perform_t_test tool to compare the sample mean against 2000.

Investigate whether an extreme value distorts association between A=[1,2,3,4,5,50] and B=[2,4,6,8,10,12]. Step 1: call detect_outliers tool on A to identify unusually extreme values. Step 2: remove outliers from A and the paired values in B to create cleaned inputs. Step 3: call calculate_correlation tool on the cleaned data to measure the linear relationship without distortion.

For load times [210,215,220,218,212,680,214,219,211,217], summarize the raw series, identify outliers, then compute a 95% confidence interval on the cleaned data. Step 1: call generate_descriptive_statistics tool to get a baseline summary of the raw series. Step 2: call detect_outliers tool to identify unusually high or low load times. Step 3: call calculate_confidence_interval tool on the cleaned data to estimate the plausible range for the mean load time.

For temperature [15,18,22,25,30] and ice cream sales [200,250,320,380,450], standardize the values, measure association, then fit a linear regression on the original scale and report the slope. Step 1: call calculate_z_scores tool to standardize the values for comparability. Step 2: call calculate_correlation tool to measure the strength and direction of the linear relationship. Step 3: call perform_linear_regression tool to estimate the slope on the original scale.

For daily returns [-0.5,0.3,0.8,-0.2,1.2,0.5,-0.1,0.9,0.4,5.8,-4.2,0.6], run a distribution audit and then compute a 95% confidence interval on the cleaned series. Step 1: call calculate_quartiles tool to locate the key points of the ordered distribution. Step 2: call calculate_iqr tool to measure the spread of the middle 50% of returns. Step 3: call detect_outliers tool to identify extreme return values. Step 4: call calculate_confidence_interval tool on the cleaned data to estimate the plausible range for the mean return.

For pain scores [3,4,3,5,4,3,4,6,3,4], verify normality, summarize the data, then test against mean 5. Step 1: call perform_normality_test tool to check whether a parametric test is appropriate. Step 2: call generate_descriptive_statistics tool to summarize the pain-score distribution. Step 3: call perform_t_test tool to compare the sample mean against 5.

For weekly revenue [42,45,43,47,50,48,52,55,53,57,60,58], smooth the series with a 3-week moving average and then estimate trend. Step 1: call calculate_moving_average tool to reduce short-term noise in the weekly series. Step 2: call perform_linear_regression tool on the smoothed series to estimate the overall revenue trend.

For paired data X=[1,2,3,4,5] and Y=[2,4,6,8,10], calculate covariance, then correlation, then fit linear regression and report R-squared. Step 1: call calculate_covariance tool to measure how X and Y vary together. Step 2: call calculate_correlation tool to quantify the strength of their linear relationship on a standardized scale. Step 3: call perform_linear_regression tool to fit the best linear model and report R-squared.

For returns [0.02,-0.01,0.03,0.015,-0.005,0.025,0.01,-0.02,0.018,0.012], assess asymmetry, tail behavior, and distribution shape. Step 1: call calculate_skewness tool to quantify asymmetry. Step 2: call calculate_kurtosis tool to assess tail weight and peakedness. Step 3: call perform_normality_test tool to test whether the distribution departs from normality.

For delivery times [24,18,36,22,19,42,21,28,17,35,20,30], understand spread and identify possible logistics outliers. Step 1: call calculate_quartiles tool to summarize the ordered spread of the delivery times. Step 2: call calculate_iqr tool to measure the spread of the middle half of the data. Step 3: call detect_outliers tool to identify unusually long or short delivery times.
